title: icon deleted from task bar
original_question: I accidently deleted an icon for microsoft solitaire from my task bar.   Is there a way to get it back?
preliminary_solution: 1. Click the Start button or press the Windows key. 2. Type "Microsoft Solitaire Collection" into the search bar. 3. When the app appears in the search results, right-click on it. 4. Select "Pin to taskbar" from the context menu.
cq_1: Can you still find and launch Microsoft Solitaire from the Start Menu or by searching for it?
user_response_1: I haven't checked. I just know the icon disappeared from the bottom of my screen.
solution_1: To restore the Microsoft Solitaire icon, first try searching for the application. Click the Start button, type "Microsoft Solitaire Collection" into the search bar, and then right-click the app in the search results. Select "Pin to taskbar" from the context menu. This method will re-add the icon if the application is still installed on your system.
cq_2: Have you noticed any other icons missing from your taskbar or desktop, or any other unusual behavior with your computer?
user_response_2: I haven't checked if anything else is missing. I just know I accidentally deleted the Solitaire icon.
solution_2: Since the issue appears isolated to the Solitaire icon, the primary step is to confirm the application's presence and then re-pin it. Please click the Start button, type "Microsoft Solitaire Collection" into the search bar, and observe if the application appears in the search results. If it does, right-click it and select "Pin to taskbar" to restore the icon.
cq_3: Could you please try searching for "Microsoft Solitaire Collection" in the Start Menu and let me know if it appears in the results?
user_response_3: I haven't checked. I just know the icon disappeared from the bottom of my screen.
final_solution: To restore your Microsoft Solitaire icon, follow these steps: 1. Click the Start button (Windows icon). 2. Type "Microsoft Solitaire Collection" into the search bar. 3. When the application appears in the search results, right-click on it. 4. Select "Pin to taskbar" from the context menu. This will place the icon back on your taskbar.
accepted_answer: Hi,  This covers how to get a Desktop Shortcut and get your Taskbar icon back:     Left click on far left Icon in the Taskbar > then in the "Start" Window click on down Arrow at bottom left to go to "Apps by Category" Window > locate the App > right click on it and select "Open file location" > in the next Window that presents itself you right click on App from the list > run Mouse Cursor over "Send to" > select "Desktop (create shortcut)".  And while you are at the "Apps by Category" Window with it > right click on the particular App > select "Pin to Taskbar"   Cheers.

title: How can I restore the old version of the calculator on Windows 8.1?
original_question: Original title: The old version of calculator I have recently upgraded from 8.0 to 8.1.  There is a new version of the calculator that I don't particularly like.  I would like to restore the old version of the calculator if I can if you help me.  Thanks.
preliminary_solution: The classic desktop calculator is typically still available in Windows 8.1. To access it:1. Go to your Desktop.2. Press the Windows Key + S to open the Search charm.3. Type "calculator" or "calc.exe" into the search bar.4. Select the "Calculator" desktop app from the search results to launch it. You can then pin it to your taskbar for easy access.
cq_1: Have you tried searching for 'calc.exe' or 'Calculator' from the desktop search to see if the classic desktop version is still available?
user_response_1: Yes, I've searched for "calc" on the Start Screen and it does find the older version I like. However, it's really frustrating to have to search for it every single time I need to use it.
solution_1: Since you've confirmed the classic calculator is found via search, you can easily make it more accessible. Simply right-click the 'Calculator' desktop app from the search results and select 'Pin to Taskbar' or 'Pin to Start'. This will allow you to launch it with a single click from your taskbar or Start Screen, eliminating the need to search every time.
cq_2: Are you encountering any issues when trying to pin the classic calculator to the taskbar or Start Screen?
user_response_2: I haven't checked. I just know I have been going to the Start Screen and searching for "calc" every single time to find the one I like.
solution_2: To avoid searching every time, you can easily pin the classic calculator. After searching for "calc" on the Start Screen and seeing the older version, right-click on its tile or icon. From the context menu, select either "Pin to Taskbar" or "Pin to Start." This will create a permanent shortcut, allowing you to launch it with a single click without needing to search again.
cq_3: Are you familiar with how to right-click on an app tile or icon on the Start Screen or in search results?
user_response_3: I'm not sure about that. I usually just search for 'calc' on the Start Screen and click directly on it.
final_solution: To easily access your preferred calculator, search for 'calc' on the Start Screen. When the classic Calculator appears, right-click its tile or icon (or press and hold if using a touchscreen). From the options that appear, select 'Pin to Taskbar' or 'Pin to Start'. This will create a permanent shortcut, allowing you to launch it with a single click without needing to search every time.
accepted_answer:
Hi,  Welcome to Microsoft community forum. There are two types of calculator on Windows 8.1, a modern one and a desktop one. You cannot restore the older version of calculator on Windows 8.1.  If you want to use classic Windows calculator follow these steps to open it: If you want to open Calculator app you can do is by searching for calculator on Modern UI. For more information on Calculator app refer this Microsoft article:  Calculator app for Windows: FLarryQ http://windows.microsoft.com/en-us/windows-8/calculator-app-faq    Hope this helps. If you need further assistance with Windows, let us know and will be glad to help.

Hi,   Thanks for the update. There is no need to search for calculator each time from start screen. You can try these steps to easily access calculator.   Step 1: Pin the calculator shortcut on Windows 8/8.1 start!     Step 2: There are times that you need to activate the Windows Calculator quickly. Instead of navigating through the Start menu, you can setup a keyboard shortcut to the application.     Now all you have to do is press the shortcut key you created, and the Calculator will appear. If you want to delete the keyboard shortcut, open the file properties dialog box click the box next to 'Shortcut key', and press the Delete key.   Hope this helps. If you need further assistance with Windows, let us know and will be glad to help.

title: Bing news crashing!!!!!!!!!!!!!!

After that it crashes immediately after opening up. I noticed on this blog other users are having the same problem. Is their a fix or update? 4-30-14, wind. 8.1
preliminary_solution: 1. Right-click the Bing News tile on your Start screen and select "Uninstall." 2. Open the Windows Store, search for "Bing News," and reinstall the app. This process will clear any corrupted customization settings that might be causing the immediate crash upon opening.
cq_1: Does the Bing News app crash immediately upon launch, or does it briefly open and then crash when attempting to load content?
user_response_1: It crashes immediately right after I try to open it. It doesn't even stay open long enough to load anything.
solution_1: The immediate crash upon opening strongly indicates corrupted app data or settings, likely stemming from your recent source customization. Reinstalling the Bing News app by uninstalling it from the Start screen and then reinstalling from the Windows Store remains the most effective solution. This process will clear any problematic local data, allowing the app to launch cleanly without attempting to load corrupted configurations.
cq_2: Have you recently installed any Windows updates or other system-level software updates around the time this issue started?
user_response_2: I'm not sure about that. I only know the crashing started today right after I added some new sources to the customize tile.
solution_2: The immediate crash after customizing sources strongly indicates corrupted app settings. Reinstalling the Bing News app by uninstalling it from the Start screen and then reinstalling from the Windows Store remains the most effective solution. This process will ensure a fresh installation, clearing any problematic local data and allowing the app to launch without attempting to load the corrupted configurations.
cq_3: When you mentioned other users having the same problem on a blog, did they also report it starting specifically after customizing sources, or was it a more general crashing issue?
user_response_3: I'm not sure about that. I just saw on the blog that they were experiencing the exact same crashing problem.
final_solution:
The immediate crash after customizing sources strongly indicates corrupted app data. Please follow these steps:
1. Right-click the Bing News tile on your Start screen and select "Uninstall."
2. Open the Windows Store, search for "Bing News," and reinstall the app.
3. After reinstalling, check for any pending Windows updates and install them to ensure system compatibility.

title: Edit Comment in Excel 2007
original_question: When editing comments in Microsoft Excel 2007 by right clicking and choosing “Edit Comments” that is usually the only task required. Something happened within the last week and now when right clicking and choosing “Edit Comments” it is unresponsive to the request, unless now choosing “Show/Hide Comment” first … then going back to choose “Edit Comments”. Why is this extra step now required, when usually it is not required and what changes needs to be made to so that “Show/Hide Comment” is no longer necessary like usual?
preliminary_solution: This often indicates a change in Excel's display settings or an interfering add-in. First, check Excel Options: go to the Office Button > Excel Options > Advanced. Under the 'Display' section, ensure 'For comments, show:' is set to 'Indicators and comments' or 'Indicators only, and comments on hover'. If that doesn't resolve it, try starting Excel in Safe Mode (hold Ctrl while opening Excel) to see if the issue persists, which would help rule out add-ins.
cq_1: Is this behavior occurring in all Excel workbooks, including newly created ones, or only in specific existing files?
user_response_1: I haven't checked if it happens in other workbooks or new ones. I've only noticed it in the spreadsheet I'm currently working on.
solution_1: Given the issue is currently isolated to a single spreadsheet, the problem might be file-specific rather than a global Excel setting or add-in. First, try repairing the workbook: go to File > Open, select the problematic file, click the arrow next to the Open button, and choose 'Open and Repair'. If that doesn't work, try copying the content (sheets) to a brand new Excel workbook. Also, re-verify the Excel Options for comments under Office Button > Excel Options > Advanced > Display section.
cq_2: Within the problematic spreadsheet, does this 'Show/Hide Comment' requirement apply to *all* comments, or only specific ones?
user_response_2: It only happens with the older comments that were already in the spreadsheet. If I add a brand new comment, I can edit it normally without doing that extra step.
solution_2: The fact that new comments can be edited normally, but older ones require the extra step, strongly suggests the issue lies with the properties or state of those specific older comment objects. Try selecting an affected older comment, right-clicking, and choosing 'Format Comment'. Check if any protection or display settings are different compared to a newly created comment. If direct editing remains problematic, a workaround could be to copy the text from the old comments, delete them, and then insert new comments with the copied text.
cq_3: Were these older comments created in Excel 2007, or could they have originated from an older version of Excel (e.g., Excel 2003 or earlier)?
user_response_3: I'm not sure about that. They are just older comments that were already in the spreadsheet.
final_solution: Since only older comments are affected, try these steps: 1. First, attempt to repair the workbook: go to File > Open, select your spreadsheet, click the arrow next to 'Open', and choose 'Open and Repair'. 2. If the issue persists, select an affected older comment, right-click, and choose 'Format Comment' to check for any unusual settings. 3. The most reliable solution is to recreate the problematic comments: copy the text from an old comment, delete it, and then insert a new comment with the copied text.
accepted_answer: Hi,   I have heard of this problem before but never seen it.   As a sensible precaution make a backup of you workbook.   Ensure the problem worksheet is the active (visible) sheet and try this VB code.   ALT+F11 to open vb editor. Right click 'ThisWorkbook' and insert module and paste the code below in on the right.   While still in VB editor, tap the F5 key to run the code, it should run very quickly so don't be surprised if nothing seems to have happened. Go back to the worksheet and test your comments.     Sub RepairComments() Dim CmntRange As Range Dim C As Range Dim Cmnt As String On Error Resume Next     Set CmntRange = ActiveSheet.Cells.SpecialCells(xlCellTypeComments)     If CmntRange Is Nothing Then         MsgBox "no comments on sheet"         Exit Sub     End If  For Each C In CmntRange     Cmnt = C.Comment.Text     C.Comment.Delete     C.AddComment     C.Comment.Text Text:=Cmnt Next End Sub

title: Start > Control Panel > System and Security > Back up your computer does not work
original_question: When I click "Back up your computer" nothing happens. I have tried all solutions I have found here and other forums. I have checked that services Block Level Backup Engine, Volume Shadow Copy and Windows Backup are all set to Automatic and are running. I have run sfc and it found no problems.  I am able to start sdclt.exe /configure in a command window and it runs and gets "Starting Windows Backup followed by Set up Backup .....and more wich seems to be OK. I just can't start it fron the control panel. Everything else on the control panel works fine. Please help, JJ
preliminary_solution: 1. Create a new local administrator user account and test if the "Back up your computer" link works from there. This helps determine if the issue is profile-specific. 2. If the issue persists, try performing a System Restore to a point before this problem began. 3. Ensure all available Windows Updates are installed, as they often contain fixes for system component issues.
cq_1: Does the issue persist if you try to launch "Back up your computer" from a newly created local administrator user account?
user_response_1: I haven't checked. I haven't tried making a new user account yet.
solution_1: Since you haven't yet, please create a new local administrator user account and test if the "Back up your computer" link works from there. This is a crucial step to determine if the issue is specific to your user profile or a system-wide problem. If it works in the new account, the problem lies within your original profile. If it persists, we'll explore system-level fixes.
cq_2: When you click "Back up your computer" in the Control Panel and nothing happens, do you see any related error messages in the Windows Event Viewer (specifically under 'Windows Logs' > 'Application' or 'System') around the time of the click?
user_response_2: Yes, I looked in the Application Log. It shows a message saying 'Backup 753 started', and then exactly 5 minutes later it says 'Backup 754 stopped'.
solution_2: The Event Viewer entries indicating 'Backup 753 started' and 'Backup 754 stopped' after 5 minutes are crucial. This suggests the backup process is initiating but then failing silently, rather than the link simply not working. We need to investigate why it's stopping. Please also proceed with creating a new local administrator account to test the link, as this will still help differentiate between a profile-specific issue and a system-wide problem.
cq_3: Are there any other error messages or warnings in the Event Viewer (Application or System logs) that occur *between* the 'Backup 753 started' and 'Backup 754 stopped' entries, or immediately preceding the 'Backup 754 stopped' entry, that might provide more detail on the cause of the failure?
user_response_3: I'm not sure about that. I only noticed the 'started' and 'stopped' entries in the log.
final_solution:
The 'Backup 753 started' and 'Backup 754 stopped' entries indicate the backup process is initiating but failing after 5 minutes. Please follow these steps:
1. Create a new local administrator account and test the "Back up your computer" link. This will confirm if the issue is profile-specific.
2. If the problem persists, ensure your intended backup destination (if configured) has sufficient free space and is accessible.
3. Run `chkdsk /f /r` on all internal hard drives to check for and repair any disk errors that might be causing the backup to fail silently.
4. If the issue continues, consider performing a repair install of Windows 7 to restore core system files without affecting your personal data.
accepted_answer: Hi,   Since how long have you had this issue?   I’d suggest you to open event viewer  and check if there are any significant error messages/codes related to the issue.   Also refer:   What information appears in event logs (Event Viewer)?   Step 1:   Try performing backup by booting the computer in a clean boot state and check if that fixes the issue.   Booting the computer in clean boot state helps in identifying if any third party applications or startup items are causing the issue. If yes, you may have to contact the program manufacturer for any updates or uninstall and re-install the program.   Refer the following Microsoft KB article for more information on how to troubleshoot a problem by performing a clean boot in Windows Vista or in Windows 7:   How to troubleshoot a problem by performing a clean boot in Windows Vista or in Windows 7 http://support.microsoft.com/kb/929135   Note:After troubleshooting, make sure to put the computer to start as usual as mentioned in  Step 7 in the above KB article.   Step 2:   Try performing backup in a new user account and check if the issue persists in new user account.   AzRhondaadeem - Microsoft Support

title: 'Skype can't connect' & 'unreponsive WiFi' issues
original_question: Dear benevolent Skype community, Trying to sign into Skype on Desktop, I have the correct username and password, but when I click 'sign in' the message 'Skpye can't connect' appears, and I can go no further and hence cannot sign into Skype. Additionally, the Skype WiFi is apparently unable to connect as the network is not responding correctly, and it asks me to try connecting to a different network. However, I am able to use the internet without any issue on other programmes. I do not know how to proceed in order to solve this problem, and I do not know if these two problems are connected. It is not clear from FAQ's how to troubleshoot this, so any help would be appreciated. I have changed location since I last signed into Skype, which may or may not be an issue for others, but I felt it worth mentioning. Any help would be appreciated! Many thanks in advance, Christopher
preliminary_solution: First, ensure you are connected to your regular internet service provider's Wi-Fi network, not any legacy 'Skype WiFi' service, which has been discontinued. If you are on your standard network, try resetting your router and modem. Next, check your firewall settings to ensure Skype is allowed to access the internet. Temporarily disabling your firewall or antivirus software might help diagnose if they are blocking the connection.
cq_1: Are you attempting to connect using a service specifically labeled 'Skype WiFi' or your standard home/office Wi-Fi network?
user_response_1: I'm trying to use the feature specifically called Skype WiFi, but it keeps saying the network isn't responding. My regular internet connection is working perfectly fine for everything else.
solution_1: The 'Skype WiFi' service has been discontinued, which is why you are unable to connect to it. Please ensure you are attempting to sign into Skype using your standard home or office Wi-Fi network, which you confirmed is working for other applications. If you still encounter the 'Skype can't connect' error while on your regular internet, try resetting your network settings or temporarily disabling your firewall/antivirus to rule out interference.
cq_2: When you try to sign into Skype using your regular internet connection, does the 'Skype can't connect' error appear immediately, or after a delay?
user_response_2: I'm not sure about that. I just know that after I put in my username and password and click sign in, the error pops up and I can't get any further.
solution_2: Since the 'Skype WiFi' service is no longer active, please ensure you are attempting to sign into Skype using your standard internet connection. If the 'Skype can't connect' error persists, try updating your Skype application to the latest version. If that doesn't resolve it, consider uninstalling and then reinstalling Skype to ensure a clean installation. Also, check your computer's proxy settings, as these can sometimes interfere with connection attempts.
cq_3: Have you tried signing into Skype on a different device (e.g., a mobile phone, web browser, or another computer) using the same account and network?
user_response_3: I haven't checked that. I've only tried logging in on my computer.
final_solution: The 'Skype WiFi' service has been discontinued, so you cannot connect using it. Please ensure you are attempting to sign into Skype using your standard internet connection. If you still see 'Skype can't connect', try these steps: 1. Attempt to sign into Skype via a web browser (web.skype.com) or on a different device to check if the issue is specific to your desktop app. 2. Update your Skype application to the latest version. 3. Temporarily disable your firewall or antivirus to see if they are blocking the connection.
accepted_answer: Try to install the currently latest Skype 7.40.0.103 version using this installer:  http://download.skype.com/msi/SkypeSetup_7.40.0.103.msi  Make note of any error message received when installing.

title: Setting will open but I cannot see them. Windows 10
original_question: For around a month my settings would disappear whenever I opened. It will still show on the task bar and still show the tab when I hover my mouse over it but when I clicked it it did nothing. I didn't want to reinstall steam because I would lose all of my data on my external hard drive. I bring this to your attention now because steam started doing the same exact thing. So now I can no longer play games I didn't create shortcuts too. If somebody knows how you can fix this, please tell me.
preliminary_solution: 1. Click the problematic application's icon on the taskbar to ensure it's focused. 2. Press Alt + Spacebar to open the window menu, then press M for Move. 3. Use your arrow keys to move the window back onto your screen. If it doesn't appear immediately, try moving it in all directions. 4. Alternatively, try pressing Windows key + Shift + Left Arrow or Windows key + Shift + Right Arrow repeatedly to cycle the window across potential display areas.
cq_1: Have you recently connected or disconnected any external monitors, or changed your display resolution settings?
user_response_1: I'm not sure about that. I haven't checked anything with external monitors or display settings.
solution_1: Since you haven't checked display settings, let's ensure the windows aren't simply off-screen due to an unrecognized display configuration. First, try the Alt + Spacebar, then M, and arrow keys method, or Windows key + Shift + Left/Right Arrow. If that doesn't work, right-click your desktop and select 'Display settings'. Check if multiple displays are detected, even if only one is physically connected, and ensure your primary display is correctly identified and set to the recommended resolution.
cq_2: When you right-click on your desktop and select 'Display settings', does that window appear correctly, or does it also disappear like the others?
user_response_2: I haven't checked. I just know that when I try to open my Settings normally, it disappears.
solution_2: It's crucial to determine if the 'Display settings' window itself is affected. Please try right-clicking on an empty area of your desktop and selecting 'Display settings'. Observe carefully if this window appears correctly, or if it also disappears like your other applications. If it disappears, immediately try the Alt + Spacebar, M, and arrow keys method, or Windows key + Shift + Left/Right Arrow to bring it into view.
cq_3: Does this disappearing window behavior occur with any other applications besides Settings and Steam, such as File Explorer or your web browser?
user_response_3: It also happens with the Microsoft Store app. I haven't checked if it happens with File Explorer or my web browser.
final_solution: This issue, affecting multiple apps like Settings, Steam, and Microsoft Store, strongly indicates windows are opening off-screen. First, try to bring them back: 1. Click the app on the taskbar. 2. Press Alt + Spacebar, then M, and use arrow keys to move the window. 3. Alternatively, press Windows key + Shift + Left/Right Arrow repeatedly. Once you can see a window, open 'Display settings' (right-click desktop) and ensure your primary display is correctly identified and set to the recommended resolution. If issues persist, consider updating or reinstalling your display drivers.
accepted_answer: show on the task bar and still show the tab when I hover my mouse over it but when I clicked it it did nothing.   Check to see if you have a window that is moved off screen (or located on another monitor).  Right-click the Taskbar icon's thumbnail when you have raised it and choose Move from the thumbnail's window menu.  Notice the change in mouse pointer but in your case you can not use it to drag.  What you can do however is use a cursor key to engage the mouse pointer on the window's title bar for a drag move.  Then drag move the window into view.  In the case where the window is on a different monitor you would also have the (simpler) alternative of using a  Win+Shift+Cursor  move.   Good luck Glenn Aldwinckle ---

title: Did copy and pasting ruins Youtube on Microsoft Edge?
original_question: I wanted check out some things by right clicking and press copy. But, the page is reloaded. I tried right clicking and press copy again and it does a same thing. I demand to know what caused the problem and why does it keep doing it. I need to know. Thank you.
preliminary_solution: 1. Clear your Microsoft Edge browser's cache and cookies. 2. Try disabling all browser extensions temporarily and then re-test the copy function on YouTube. 3. Test if the issue persists when using an InPrivate window in Edge. These steps help rule out corrupted data or conflicting add-ons.
cq_1: Does this page reloading issue occur only on YouTube, or do you experience it on other websites when you try to copy text or images?
user_response_1: It only happens on YouTube. I tried going to other websites and copying things, but it works perfectly fine there.
solution_1: Since the issue is specific to YouTube, focus on YouTube-related troubleshooting within Edge. Clear your browser's cache and cookies, specifically for YouTube. Temporarily disable any browser extensions that might interact with YouTube. Also, try accessing YouTube in an InPrivate window to see if the problem persists, which can help rule out profile-specific data corruption.
cq_2: Does this page reloading issue occur regardless of whether you are logged into your YouTube account, or does it only happen when you are signed in?
user_response_2: I'm not sure about that. I haven't checked if being logged in or out makes a difference.
solution_2: To further diagnose the YouTube-specific reloading issue, please test if the problem occurs when you are logged out of your YouTube account. If it only happens when logged in, try clearing YouTube-specific site data and cookies, and temporarily disable any extensions that might interact with your YouTube account. This will help determine if the issue is tied to your account settings or a conflict with a logged-in state.
cq_3: When you right-click to copy on YouTube, does the page reload immediately upon right-clicking, or only after you select the 'Copy' option from the context menu?
user_response_3: It happens right after I click on the copy option. Just right-clicking by itself doesn't make the page reload.
final_solution: Your YouTube copy issue likely stems from corrupted site data or an extension conflict. First, clear all YouTube-specific site data and cookies in Microsoft Edge settings. Next, test copying while logged out of YouTube and also in an InPrivate window. Finally, ensure your Microsoft Edge browser is fully updated to the latest version. If the problem persists, temporarily disable all browser extensions.
accepted_answer: Have you tried using the short keys instead? Highlight what you wanted to copy and then  press Ctrl + C to copy, and then Ctrl + V to paste.  If you have and still doesn't work, what you can do for the meantime is click on the 3 dots at the upper right corner and select Open with Internet Explorer. Then, try copy & paste again.  Let us know.

title: VLOOKUP for time stamp sometimes returns incorrect value?
original_question: I have a table with a column of time stamps. These come from an external source and the cell is configured as "custom h:mm". I have created a 2nd table with deduplicated values of the time stamps. Then I am using a VLOOKUP function to match the values from the deduplicated table to the original one. Some of the return values are correct, while others are incorrect, and I don't understand why. I've tried to use VALUE function and change the columns to another form of data other than time, but it did not work. This is an example from the original table. Column A is the time stamps and column B is the return values from the second table (below).   0:11 11 1:06 18 1:09 21 1:11 23 2:06 15 2:05 29 1:03 11 2:10 34 1:02 11 2:06 15 2:06 15 6:06 78 3:05 14 This is the second table I have created: 0:11 11 1:06 18 1:09 21 1:11 23 2:06 30 2:05 29 1:03 15 2:10 34 1:02 14 6:06 78 3:05 41 As you can see, values like 0:11, 2:05 and 6:06 return correctly, while values for 2:06 and 3:05 do not. I have no idea why this is happening. Any ideas? P.S. The source of the original time stamp comes from a very old external software and there is no way to reimport the data differently.
preliminary_solution: 1. Modify your VLOOKUP formula to explicitly use FALSE or 0 as the fourth argument to ensure an exact match. For example, =VLOOKUP(lookup_value, table_array, col_index_num, FALSE). 2. If the problem continues, the time values might have hidden decimal differences. Create a helper column in both your original and lookup tables using =TEXT(cell,"h:mm") to convert times to text strings, then perform the VLOOKUP on these text columns.
cq_1: Could you confirm if you are using FALSE or 0 as the fourth argument in your VLOOKUP function to ensure an exact match?
user_response_1: I'm not sure about that. I just know I'm using a VLOOKUP to match the timestamps, but I haven't checked what the fourth part of the formula is.
solution_1: The VLOOKUP function's fourth argument is crucial for exact matches. Please ensure your VLOOKUP formula explicitly uses `FALSE` or `0` as the fourth argument (e.g., `=VLOOKUP(lookup_value, table_array, col_index_num, FALSE)`). If this doesn't resolve the issue, it's likely due to hidden decimal differences in the time values. In that case, convert both your original and lookup time columns to text strings using `=TEXT(cell,"h:mm")` in helper columns, then perform the VLOOKUP on these new text columns.
cq_2: When you tried using the VALUE function or changing data types, did you observe any changes in the underlying numerical values of the time stamps (e.g., by formatting the cells as 'General' or 'Number')?
user_response_2: I'm not sure about that. I tried changing the format to something other than time, but I didn't really look at the underlying numbers.
solution_2: Since you haven't confirmed the VLOOKUP's fourth argument, please ensure it's set to `FALSE` or `0` for an exact match. If issues persist, the problem likely stems from hidden decimal differences in the time stamps. To verify this, format a few problematic time cells (e.g., '2:06' from both tables) as 'General' or 'Number' to reveal their true numerical values. First, ensure your VLOOKUP formula uses `FALSE` or `0` as the fourth argument for an exact match. If the problem persists, create helper columns in both your original and lookup tables. Choose “Document Map” style under the Styles pull-down menu. Choose the styles/fonts/sizes that you want. Click Apply.  Comment Balloons:  Have comment balloons displayed and then highlight some text from any comment. Select Format/Styles from the Word menu. Choose the ‘Comment Text’ style. For the Navigation Pane, open the pane, go to Format > Styles, select 'Document Map' style, adjust font/size, and apply. For Comment Balloons, display comments, highlight text in a comment, go to Format > Styles, select 'Comment Text' style, make changes, and apply. This method should consistently work.

If icons are still missing, rebuild the icon cache: Open Command Prompt as administrator and run 'ie4uinit.exe -show', then 'taskkill /IM explorer.exe /F', then 'DEL "%localappdata%\IconCache.db" /A', and finally 'start explorer.exe'.

title: Highlight the answer in a column
original_question: I have a table of data. It is several hundred rows. I show the min, max and mean of a particular column. Is there a way, in addition to getting the answer, to highlight (different color or background) where it is in the column? It would save time trying to find it. Thanks.
preliminary_solution: You can achieve this using Conditional Formatting. Select the column, then go to Home > Conditional Formatting > New Rule. For Min/Max, use "Format only top or bottom ranked values" and select "Bottom 1" and "Top 1" respectively. For the mean, use "Use a formula to determine which cells to format" with a formula like `=A1=AVERAGE($A:$A)` (adjust A1 to your first data cell and $A:$A to your column range). Apply different formats for each rule.
cq_1: For the mean, are you looking to highlight cells that contain the exact calculated mean value, or cells that are closest to the mean?
user_response_1: I'm not sure about that. I just want the cell in the data column that matches the calculated answer to change to a different color.
solution_1: To highlight the min, max, and exact mean values in your column, use Conditional Formatting. For the minimum and maximum, select the column, go to Home > Conditional Formatting > Top/Bottom Rules, and choose "Bottom 1 Item" and "Top 1 Item" respectively, applying different formats. For the exact mean, use a "New Rule" with the option "Use a formula to determine which cells to format." The formula should be `=A1=AVERAGE($A:$A)` (adjust A1 to your first data cell and $A:$A to your column range), applying a distinct format.
cq_2: If the minimum, maximum, or calculated mean value appears multiple times in the column, should all occurrences be highlighted, or only the first one found?
user_response_2: I'm not sure about that. I haven't really thought about what should happen if the number shows up more than once.
solution_2: The Conditional Formatting rules for minimum, maximum, and exact mean will highlight all cells in the column that match these calculated values. This approach ensures that every instance of the min, max, or exact mean within your data is visually identified, which is generally helpful for quickly locating them. You will apply separate rules for each, allowing for distinct highlighting.
cq_3: Would you prefer to use different highlight colors or background fills for the minimum, maximum, and mean values, or should they all be highlighted with the same visual style?
user_response_3: I'm not sure about that. I just want the cells to change to a different color or background so I don't have to manually search through hundreds of rows to find them.
final_solution: To highlight the minimum, maximum, and exact mean values in your column, use Conditional Formatting. Select your data column, then apply three separate rules: 1. For the minimum, go to Home > Conditional Formatting > Top/Bottom Rules > Bottom 1 Item. 2. For the maximum, use Top/Bottom Rules > Top 1 Item. 3. For the exact mean, create a New Rule using a formula: `=A1=AVERAGE($A:$A)` (adjust A1 to your first data cell and $A:$A to your column range). Apply a distinct fill color or font style for each rule to easily differentiate them.
accepted_answer: I think Aaron means, in the cells in the column with numbers, add three conditional formatting rules to each cell. You can create the rules in one cell and then copy...paste special...formats to the other cells in the column.   One rule checks if the cell's value is equal to the cell that contains the MIN of the column and applies the color; the second rule checks if the cell's value is equal to the cell that contains the MAX of the column and applies that color; the third does the same for the average.  So only two to three cells would be colored in the column of numbers.

title: Bullets in the Table for PowerPoint
original_question: Hi, I am struggling to figure out why when I press the bullet button to format the texts in the table, the bullet only formats the first line break. For example, if I copy an excel text from a cell with line breaks like this: Apple Oranges Cabbage and paste it into a PowerPoint table cell and press bullet, only the first bullet will have a bullet while the others have hanging indents like this •      Apple Oranges Cabbages I would like the text within the table cell to look like this •       Apple •       Oranges •       Cabbages Currently, I have to manually create the bullets by erasing the pre-made line breaks from excel and pressing enter. However, this can get tedious. Is there a way to fix this problem? Thank you
preliminary_solution: After pasting the text into the PowerPoint table cell, select the text and use the 'Replace' function (Ctrl+H). In the 'Find what' field, type '^l' (caret L) to find line breaks. In the 'Replace with' field, type '^p' (caret P) to replace them with paragraph breaks. Click 'Replace All', then apply your bullet formatting.
cq_1: Are you using a specific 'Paste Special' option when bringing the text from Excel into PowerPoint, or just a standard paste (Ctrl+V)?
user_response_1: I'm not sure about that. I just select the Excel cell and paste it into the PowerPoint table normally.
solution_1: Since you're using a standard paste, PowerPoint interprets Excel's line breaks as soft returns within a single paragraph. To fix this, after pasting, select the text in the table cell. Use the 'Replace' function (Ctrl+H) to find '^l' (line breaks) and replace them with '^p' (paragraph breaks). This will convert each line into its own paragraph, allowing the bullet formatting to apply correctly to each item.
cq_2: What version of PowerPoint are you currently using (e.g., PowerPoint 365, 2019, 2016)?
user_response_2: I'm not sure about that. I just know it's Microsoft PowerPoint.
solution_2: The most effective way to achieve the desired bullet formatting is to use the 'Replace' function after pasting. Select the text within the table cell, then open the 'Replace' dialog (Ctrl+H). In 'Find what', type '^l' (lowercase L) to target line breaks, and in 'Replace with', type '^p' to convert them into paragraph breaks. This will ensure each item becomes a separate paragraph, allowing the bullet points to apply individually.
cq_3: Does this issue only occur when copying text from Excel, or do you experience the same problem when pasting text with line breaks from other sources (e.g., a text editor like Notepad) into PowerPoint tables?
user_response_3: I haven't checked if it happens with other programs like Notepad. I've only noticed it when copying my tables over from Excel.
final_solution: To correctly apply bullets to each item, after pasting from Excel, select the text in the PowerPoint table cell. Use the 'Replace' function (Ctrl+H) to find '^l' (line breaks) and replace them with '^p' (paragraph breaks). This converts each item into a separate paragraph, allowing bullets to apply individually. For future pastes, consider using 'Paste Special' and selecting 'Keep Text Only' to avoid formatting issues.
accepted_answer: Hi Deborah Park,  Based on your description, when you click the Bullet option, only the first text are formatted with the bullet. I would like to suggest you ensure you have selected the whole text, and select the bullet in PowerPoint. You can find more details in this article: Add bullets or numbers to text  If the issue persists, could you please provide detailed steps and related screenshots about how you copy an excel text from a cell with line breaks and encounter the issue, so we can better understand your situation and help you?  Regards, YogaStephanie

title: export to CD option in ppt2016
original_question: The embedded music doesn't play on powerpoint 2016 when I exported it to a memory stick-  I used mp3's '
preliminary_solution: 1. Open your presentation in PowerPoint 2016. 2. Go to File > Export > Package Presentation for CD > Package for CD. 3. In the 'Package for CD' dialog box, click 'Options.' 4. Ensure 'Linked files' is checked under 'Files to be included.' 5. Choose 'Copy to Folder' and select your memory stick as the destination. Verify the created folder contains both the PPTX and the MP3 files.

user_response_1: Yes, it works perfectly on my computer. The music plays just fine before I put it on the memory stick.
solution_1: Since the music plays correctly on your computer, the issue likely stems from the export process or how the presentation is launched from the memory stick. When using 'Package Presentation for CD,' ensure you select 'Copy to Folder' and verify that both the .pptx file and the embedded .mp3 files are present in the destination folder on the memory stick. Also, confirm that the presentation is being opened using the included PowerPoint Viewer (if applicable) or a compatible version of PowerPoint on the target machine, maintaining the relative file paths.
cq_2: When you attempt to play the presentation from the memory stick, are you opening the .pptx file directly with PowerPoint 2016 installed on another computer, or are you using the 'PowerPoint Viewer' application that is included when you use the 'Package Presentation for CD' option?
user_response_2: I'm just opening the file normally on the second computer, which has PowerPoint 2013 installed. I'm not sure about a PowerPoint Viewer application.
solution_2: The problem likely stems from compatibility differences between PowerPoint 2016 and PowerPoint 2013, or how media is handled across versions. When packaging the presentation, ensure all linked MP3 files are correctly copied to the destination folder on the memory stick. Before exporting, try using the 'Optimize Media Compatibility' feature in PowerPoint 2016 (File > Info) to convert media to a more universally compatible format, which often resolves playback issues on older PowerPoint versions.
cq_3: When you open the presentation on the computer with PowerPoint 2013, do you see any error messages related to media, or does the music simply not play without any specific notification?
user_response_3: I don't get any pop-up error messages when I open it, the music just doesn't play. However, if I put my mouse over the audio icon, a little box says 'cant link to music'.
final_solution: The 'can't link to music' error indicates the MP3 files are not correctly found by PowerPoint 2013. To resolve this, use PowerPoint 2016's 'Package Presentation for CD' feature. Select 'Copy to Folder' and choose your memory stick as the destination. Crucially, verify that the resulting folder on the memory stick contains both your .pptx file and all the .mp3 music files. Always open the presentation from within this packaged folder to ensure all links are maintained.
accepted_answer: Try this:  Delete the existing music files Put the music files in the same folder as the PowerPoint file Re-insert them  That generally produces "pathless" links that work on other systems so long as:  - The PPT/x and music files stay together in the same folder and - The computer is capable of playing the music files in the first place  But something else to check on ... see, it's odd that PPT should be complaining about linked files. The default nowadays is for it to embed music and video files, not link them, unless you explicitly told it to link (in which case, re-insert them, DON'T link and you should be ok).  Unless perhaps you're playing the presentation on an older version of PowerPoint; it might not be able to play embedded MP3s.  Something else to check ...

title: powerpoint 2016
original_question: After completing all animations in a given slide (PP 2016), how do I "rewind" back to the original position of the slide - i.e., the beginning.
preliminary_solution: If you are in presentation mode, to reset the current slide's animations to their starting state: 1. Press the 'P' key (or Left Arrow) to navigate to the previous slide. 2. Immediately press the 'N' key (or Right Arrow) to return to the current slide. This action will restart all animations on the current slide from the beginning.
cq_1: Are you trying to reset the slide's animations during a live presentation, or while you are editing the slide in design view?
user_response_1: I'm not sure about that. I just want the slide to go back to the starting point right after the animations finish playing.
solution_1: After all animations on a slide have finished playing, to "rewind" the slide back to its initial state for replaying animations, you need to navigate away from the slide and then return to it. In presentation mode, press 'P' (or Left Arrow) to go to the previous slide, then 'N' (or Right Arrow) to return to the current slide. This action will reset all animations on that slide, making them ready to play from the beginning again.
cq_2: Do you want the slide to automatically revert to its initial visual state after animations complete, or are you looking for a way to manually reset the animations for replay?
user_response_2: I'm not sure about that. I just want the slide to go back to its original starting point right after the animations finish playing.
solution_2: PowerPoint animations typically leave objects in their final animated state. The manual method of pressing 'P' then 'N' (or left/right arrow keys) during a presentation will reset the animation sequence, allowing you to replay all animations from the start. If you want objects to visually return to their original positions after animating, this usually requires setting up specific exit animations or duplicating slides to create a 'reset' visual state.
cq_3: When you say 'go back to its original starting point,' do you mean you want the animated objects to visually return to their initial positions on the slide, or do you want the animation sequence to be ready to play from the beginning again?
user_response_3: I want the objects to visually return to where they started. I just want to see them in their original positions after all the moving finishes.
final_solution:
PowerPoint animations typically leave objects in their final animated state. To make objects visually return to their original positions after animations finish, you have two main options:

1. **Add Exit Animations:** For each animated object, add an 'Exit' animation (e.g., 'Fly Out' or 'Disappear') that brings it back to its starting position or makes it vanish. Ensure these are timed to play after the main animations.
2. **Duplicate Slide:** Duplicate your slide. On the duplicated slide, remove all animations so it shows objects in their original positions. Set the original slide to automatically advance to this duplicate after its animations complete.
accepted_answer: You could try:  Insert a blank slide before the animated slide and give it an auto transition time of after zero seconds.  If you have a button on the animated slide to go to previous it should work.  You can also do this with code if you know how to use it. Sub resetMe() Dim CSP As Long CSP = SlideShowWindows(1).View.CurrentShowPosition SlideShowWindows(1).View.GotoSlide (CSP), True End Sub

cq_2: Are you using a personal computer at home, or is this a device provided by your employer or educational institution?

title: I have a data sheet that I need to Fill out - Excel 2010
original_question: I have a data sheet that I will be filling out often. I am trying to set up the formulas so that when the title or pc of equipment in entered from a drop down list, 3 other cells will automatically populate. Sheet 1 A          |       B        |             C              |          D      | Lift #    |  Serial #    |   Manufacturer    |  Model #   | When I enter info for Column A (116 possibilities currently) from a drop down menu, I want B, C, and D to populate Sheet 2 A          |       B        |             C              |          D      | Lift #    |  Serial #    |   Manufacturer    |  Model #   | --------------------------------------------------------- Lift 1   | 32323232 |  Sunset Blvd         |  32SBGT    | ....and you can multiply this type of information by 116, but the list does grow every year as some equipment is added. I have tried to use Vlookup, If Value, HLookup. I am clearly missing something. Is there a single formula, a multiple formula? Something where this will continue down the entire workbook as at the end of each year, I could literally have well over 10,000 lines and I do need this information to populate. I am not going to be the only one using this document, and I do not want errors from people typing in and misreading documentation Thank you!!
preliminary_solution:
You can achieve this using VLOOKUP formulas. Assuming your master data is on 'Sheet 2' with 'Lift #' in Column A, 'Serial #' in B, 'Manufacturer' in C, and 'Model #' in D:
1. In Sheet 1, for the 'Serial #' cell (e.g., B2), enter: `=VLOOKUP(A2, 'Sheet 2'!A:D, 2, FALSE)`
2. For 'Manufacturer' (e.g., C2), enter: `=VLOOKUP(A2, 'Sheet 2'!A:D, 3, FALSE)`
3. For 'Model #' (e.g., D2), enter: `=VLOOKUP(A2, 'Sheet 2'!A:D, 4, FALSE)`
Drag these formulas down to apply them to all subsequent rows.
cq_1: Could you confirm the exact sheet name and range where your master equipment data (Lift #, Serial #, Manufacturer, Model #) is stored, and if the dropdown list in Sheet 1, Column A, directly references the 'Lift #' column from that master data sheet?
user_response_1: The master data is on Sheet 2, but I'm not sure about the exact range since the list keeps growing. Yes, the drop-down in Sheet 1 is for the Lift # from that master list.
solution_1: To accommodate your growing master data list, ensure your VLOOKUP formulas reference entire columns on 'Sheet 2'. For example, in Sheet 1, cell B2, use `=IFERROR(VLOOKUP(A2, 'Sheet 2'!A:D, 2, FALSE), "")`. Apply similar formulas for columns C and D, adjusting the column index (3 and 4 respectively). This setup will dynamically pull data as new entries are added to 'Sheet 2' without needing to update the formula ranges, and `IFERROR` will prevent #N/A errors.
cq_2: How is the dropdown list in Sheet 1, Column A, currently populated? Is it a static list, or does it dynamically update to reflect new 'Lift #' entries added to your master data on Sheet 2?
user_response_2: I'm not sure about that. I just know there are 116 possibilities in the drop-down right now, and the list grows every year as we add new equipment.
solution_2: To ensure your dropdown list in Sheet 1, Column A, automatically updates with new equipment, convert your master data on Sheet 2 into an Excel Table (Insert > Table). Then, for your dropdown list source, reference the 'Lift #' column of this table. The VLOOKUP formulas you've already implemented will then correctly pull data for both existing and newly added equipment, as both the dropdown and the VLOOKUP range will dynamically expand.
cq_3: Could you describe the steps you took to create the dropdown list in Sheet 1, Column A? For example, did you go to Data > Data Validation, and what did you enter in the 'Source' field?
user_response_3: I'm not sure about that. The drop-down list is already set up with the 116 equipment options, but I don't remember the exact steps used to create it.
final_solution:
To ensure your system works seamlessly with growing data:
1. Convert your master data on 'Sheet 2' into an Excel Table (select data, then Insert > Table). Name it, e.g., 'EquipmentData'.
2. Update your dropdown list in Sheet 1, Column A: Go to Data > Data Validation, select 'List' for 'Allow', and for 'Source', enter `=INDIRECT("EquipmentData[Lift #]")`.
3. Use these VLOOKUP formulas in Sheet 1, starting from B2: `=IFERROR(VLOOKUP(A2, EquipmentData, 2, FALSE), "")` for Serial #, `=IFERROR(VLOOKUP(A2, EquipmentData, 3, FALSE), "")` for Manufacturer, and `=IFERROR(VLOOKUP(A2, EquipmentData, 4, FALSE), "")` for Model #. Drag these down. This setup will dynamically update everything as new equipment is added.
accepted_answer: This VLOOKUP should work then (assuming Sheet 2 is actually called "Sheet 2" with a space before the 2. In cell B2, enter this:  =IFERROR(VLOOKUP(A2,'Sheet 2'!A:D,2,FALSE),"")  In C2:   =IFERROR(VLOOKUP(A2,'Sheet 2'!A:D,3,FALSE),"")  And in D2:  =IFERROR(VLOOKUP(A2,'Sheet 2'!A:D,3,FALSE),"")  The IFERROR function shows a blank in each cell if nothing is picked in the dropdowns in column A.  You'll just need to copy the formulas down the columns.

title: Text Highlight Tool is not present in PowerPoint 2016
original_question: The text highlight tool does not appear on PowerPoint 2016 in my computer so there is no way for me to highlight any relevant text. It however shows up on my laptop (PowerPoint 2016) and appears on my other Office 2016 apps such as Word. Is there a way to get the tool on my Home ribbon? Any help will be appreciated.
preliminary_solution: 1. Right-click on the PowerPoint ribbon and select "Customize the Ribbon."2. In the dialog, ensure "Home (Tabs)" is selected on the right.3. Under "Choose commands from:", select "All Commands" and look for "Text Highlight Color."4. If found, add it to a new group on your Home tab. If not, or if this doesn't resolve it, perform an Online Repair of your Office 2016 installation via Control Panel > Programs and Features.
cq_1: Could you confirm if you've customized the ribbon in PowerPoint 2016 on the computer where the Text Highlight Tool is missing?
user_response_1: I'm not sure about that. I haven't checked.
solution_1: Please begin by checking your PowerPoint ribbon customization. Right-click on the ribbon, select 'Customize the Ribbon,' and look for 'Text Highlight Color' under 'All Commands.' If you find it, add it to a new group on your Home tab. If the tool is not listed or adding it doesn't make it appear, then proceed with an Online Repair of your Office 2016 installation via Control Panel > Programs and Features.
cq_2: After checking the 'Customize the Ribbon' options, did you find 'Text Highlight Color' listed under 'All Commands'?
user_response_2: I haven't checked. I only looked at the Home ribbon where it usually is.
solution_2: Please start by explicitly checking the 'Customize the Ribbon' options. Right-click on the PowerPoint ribbon, select 'Customize the Ribbon,' and then under 'Choose commands from:' select 'All Commands.' Carefully look for 'Text Highlight Color' in that list. If you find it, add it to a new group on your Home tab. If it's not listed there, or if adding it doesn't resolve the issue, then proceed with an Online Repair of your Office 2016 installation.
cq_3: Could you please perform the 'Customize the Ribbon' check as described and let me know if 'Text Highlight Color' appears in the 'All Commands' list?
user_response_3: I haven't checked. I'm not sure about that.
final_solution: Since checking ribbon customization hasn't been performed, the most effective solution is to repair your Office 2016 installation. Go to Control Panel > Programs and Features, find Microsoft Office 2016, right-click, and select 'Change' then 'Online Repair.' This will reinstall any missing components, including the Text Highlight Tool.
accepted_answer: Text Highlighting has been possible in WORD for a very long time but it was only added to PPT in the latest Subscription version.

title: Outlook 2013 program is missing the normal window border.
original_question: How do you add the window border back to the Outlook program so it's easier to adjust the size of the window. Right now the border width is so tiny I have to hover back and forth to find the sweet spot to adjust it. Excel has the same program, no border! I want the same size border as an internet window or a folder window. About 1/8".
preliminary_solution: This sounds like a system-wide display setting. Try adjusting the window border thickness through Windows Personalization settings. Right-click on your desktop, select 'Personalize', then 'Window Color' (or 'Colors' depending on your Windows version), and look for 'Advanced appearance settings'. From there, you should be able to find an option to adjust the 'Border Padding' or 'Active Window Border' size.
cq_1: Does this thin border issue affect other non-Microsoft Office applications or only Outlook and Excel?
user_response_1: It seems to only affect my Office programs, including Word and PowerPoint too. My internet browser and regular folder windows still have normal-sized borders.
solution_1: Since the thin border issue is specific to Office programs, the problem is likely related to Office display settings or installation. First, try disabling hardware graphics acceleration within an Office application (File > Options > Advanced > Display). If that doesn't resolve it, ensure Office 2013 is fully updated. As a last resort, consider performing an online repair of your Office installation via the Control Panel to fix any corrupted files.
cq_2: Has this thin border issue always been present in your Office programs, or did it start recently after a specific update or software installation?
user_response_2: It hasn't always been like this. It started right after my computer was updated to Windows 7 and Office 2013.
solution_2: Given the issue started immediately after upgrading to Windows 7 and Office 2013, it's highly probable that display drivers or compatibility settings are at fault. Ensure your graphics drivers are fully updated for Windows 7. Also, check Office display options for any scaling settings that might be misinterpreting the window borders. If the problem persists, an online repair of Office 2013 remains a strong recommendation to address any potential installation inconsistencies from the upgrade.
cq_3: Have you checked for and installed the latest display/graphics drivers specifically compatible with Windows 7 since the upgrade?
user_response_3: I haven't checked. I'm not really sure what display drivers are or how to update them.
final_solution: The most likely cause is outdated display drivers. To update them: 1. Go to Device Manager (search in Start Menu). 2. Expand 'Display adapters', right-click your graphics card, and select 'Update driver software'. Choose 'Search automatically for updated driver software'. If that doesn't work, visit your computer manufacturer's website for Windows 7 compatible drivers. If the issue persists, try disabling hardware graphics acceleration in Office (File > Options > Advanced > Display) and perform an online repair of Office 2013 via Control Panel.
accepted_answer: This is apparently a feature, not a bug.  Please see these two articles.  https://excel.uservoice.com/forums/304921-excel-for-windows-desktop-application/suggestions/9334494-can-you-please-give-us-back-a-window-border-on-off  https://support.microsoft.com/en-us/help/2821007/how-to-disable-shadow-hwnds-in-office-2013-applications   I'm also moving your question to the Office forum, where Office experts may perhaps be aware of a method to deal with this issue.

title: Switch to new tabs in Edge
original_question: IE contains an option to switch to newly created tabs.  I always browse with this option on (why would you create a new tab an not want to look at it?).  How can I do the same in Edge? PS - could not find a category for Edge in the classification options. ***Post moved by the moderator to the appropriate forum category.***
preliminary_solution: 1. Open Microsoft Edge Settings (click the three dots menu in the top right, then 'Settings'). 2. In the left-hand menu, select 'Appearance'. 3. Scroll down to the 'Customize toolbar' section. 4. Look for an option like 'Open new tabs in the foreground' or 'Switch to new tabs immediately' and ensure it is enabled.
cq_1: Are you opening new tabs by clicking links (e.g., right-click 'Open link in new tab') or by using the '+' button/Ctrl+T?
user_response_1: I am opening them by right-clicking links and choosing to open them in a new tab. I usually do this from my search results page.
solution_1: Since you are opening new tabs by right-clicking links and selecting "Open link in new tab," the setting to switch to these tabs immediately is crucial. Navigate to Edge Settings > Appearance, and look for an option related to "Open new tabs in the foreground" or "Switch to new tabs immediately." Ensure this setting is enabled to automatically focus on newly opened tabs from links.
cq_2: When you right-click a link and choose "Open link in new tab," does the new tab open in the background, or does it not open at all?
user_response_2: The new tab does open in the background. It just leaves me looking at my current page instead of switching me over to the new one.
solution_2: Given that new tabs are opening in the background, the primary solution is to enable the setting that forces Edge to switch to new tabs immediately. Go to Edge Settings, then navigate to 'Appearance'. Look for an option like 'Open new tabs in the foreground' or 'Switch to new tabs immediately' and ensure it is toggled on. This should resolve the issue of new tabs opening in the background when clicked from links.
cq_3: Have you been able to locate and enable the 'Open new tabs in the foreground' or similar setting within Edge's Appearance settings?
user_response_3: No, I haven't been able to find that setting. I looked around for an option like the one Internet Explorer has, but I couldn't find it anywhere in Edge.
final_solution: It appears that Microsoft Edge does not have a direct setting to automatically switch to new tabs opened via 'Right-click > Open link in new tab,' as this action typically opens tabs in the background. To achieve your desired behavior, use **Ctrl + Shift + Click** on a link to open it in a new tab and immediately switch to it. Alternatively, after right-clicking and opening a link in a new background tab, press **Ctrl + Tab** to quickly switch to the newly opened tab.
accepted_answer: Thank you for clarifying. What you can do is to hold Ctrl + Shift and click on the link and it will automatically switch to the new tab.  Having this option is a good idea, we suggest that you submit your query using the Feedback Hub on this link. We consider suggestions and comments provided by our users. These feedback are being collated, reviewed, and can be considered for future updates.  Let us know if you need further assistance.

title: Linking cells in a table across multiple worksheets
original_question: So I have an excel document that has 100+ worksheets in it. We use this to list all of the various materials that we need to purchase for a project. For example, if we are using 5 different sizes of screws, we would list all of these out and then have a total quantity needed cell beside it.  Most of these sheets are identically set up in the same manner, with the exception that the name of the sheet is different and the various quantities will vary on each sheet. On our main page (Master BOM), we type out all of the different items and then we go onto the various worksheets (UNIT NAMES) that use those products. On these pages we type the quantity we need and we have it set up to where all of these sheet quantities are totaled on the Master BOM sheet. What I am now trying to do is create another sheet that lists the names of all of the worksheets and then beside these names list the total number of units being made. For example, if we are building 3 different sized boxes named Box A, Box B, and Box C, and we are ordering 2,4, & 6, respectively, I have a cell labeled Box A and the cell beside it has a quantity of 2 in it. Make sense? So I have my list completely done and the quantities listed beside them. What I am needing to do is have that corresponding quantity automatically link to the worksheet for that particular unit. So using my example above, I have Box A listed with 2 quantities. I want that 2 (or whatever number I have to update it to) to automatically link to the worksheet that I have for Box A (and yes the worksheet is labeled as Box A already). Is there a way to do this without me having to go through every single sheet and type that it is equal to a certain cell? I do have Kutools if that helps.
preliminary_solution: 1. On your 'Unit Quantities' sheet, ensure column A contains the exact names of your unit worksheets (e.g., 'Box A', 'Box B') and column B contains their respective quantities.2. Go to the first unit worksheet (e.g., 'Box A').3. In the cell where you want the total quantity to appear (e.g., A1), enter the following formula: `=VLOOKUP(RIGHT(CELL("filename",A1),LEN(CELL("filename",A1))-FIND("]",CELL("filename",A1))),'Unit Quantities'!A:B,2,FALSE)`.4. Copy this formula, then select all other unit worksheets (click the first sheet tab, then hold Shift and click the last sheet tab), and paste the formula into the *same cell* on all selected sheets. This will dynamically pull the correct quantity for each sheet.
cq_1: What specific cell (e.g., A1, B5) on each individual unit worksheet (like 'Box A' or 'Box B') should display the total number of units?
user_response_1: I'm not sure about that. I didn't write down the exact cell letter and number, but you can see how it's set up in the sample file I shared.
solution_1: Since the exact cell for the total quantity on each unit worksheet is not specified, you can choose any suitable cell (e.g., A1 or B1) on each unit sheet. The formula `=VLOOKUP(RIGHT(CELL("filename",A1),LEN(CELL("filename",A1))-FIND("]",CELL("filename",A1))),'Unit Quantities'!A:B,2,FALSE)` should be entered into your chosen cell on the first unit sheet. Then, copy this cell, select all other unit worksheets, and paste it into the *same chosen cell* on those sheets. This will dynamically link the total quantity from your 'Unit Quantities' sheet to the corresponding unit worksheet.
cq_2: On your individual unit worksheets (e.g., 'Box A'), is there a specific area or an existing label where you would prefer this total number of units to be displayed?
user_response_2: I'm not sure about that. I just know I need a specific cell on each individual sheet to automatically match the quantity on the summary sheet so I don't have to do it manually.
solution_2: Since you don't have a specific cell in mind, you can choose any empty cell on your first unit worksheet (e.g., A1, B1, or C1) to display the total quantity. Enter the provided VLOOKUP formula into that chosen cell. Then, copy this cell, select all other unit worksheets, and paste it into the *exact same cell* on each of those sheets. This method ensures the quantity from your 'Unit Quantities' sheet automatically links to the corresponding unit worksheet without manual entry.
cq_3: Are there any cells on your individual unit worksheets (e.g., 'Box A') that are already populated with critical data in the top-left corner (e.g., A1, B1, C1) that we should avoid placing this linked quantity in?
user_response_3: I'm not sure about that. I haven't checked.
final_solution: 1. On your 'Unit Quantities' sheet, ensure column A contains exact worksheet names (e.g., 'Box A') and column B has their quantities. 2. On your first unit worksheet (e.g., 'Box A'), choose an empty cell (e.g., A1 or B1) to display the total quantity. 3. Enter this formula into that chosen cell: `=VLOOKUP(RIGHT(CELL("filename",A1),LEN(CELL("filename",A1))-FIND("]",CELL("filename",A1))),'Unit Quantities'!A:B,2,FALSE)`. 4. Copy this cell, then select all other unit worksheets (click first sheet tab, hold Shift, click last sheet tab), and paste into the *same chosen cell* on all selected sheets. This will automatically link the quantities.
accepted_answer: That's great The snip shows the formula to use Note depending on what is in Takeoff you will need to adjust the range: =VLOOKUP(D2,Takeoff!B4:C20,2,FALSE)  With lots of worksheets it would be a chore do enter a formula in everyone. The trick is to group the worksheets first: 1) click on the BoxA tab 2)Hold down SHIFT and click on thtab of the last worksheet needing the formula; the tabs will now all be bright. 3)Type the formula; each worksheet will get a copy 4) Right click the tab of BoxA and select Ungroup   Hope this gets you on the right road

title: Audio Skype Android
original_question: Hi. Recently on my Samsung Galaxy S6 when i use my microphone people dont hear my voice. But when i connect my micrphone to my laptop they can hear me. What I should do to solve that? A quick answer would be awesome.
preliminary_solution: 1. Go to your phone's Settings > Apps > Skype > Permissions and ensure "Microphone" is enabled. 2. Clear Skype's cache and data by going to Settings > Apps > Skype > Storage, then select "Clear Cache" and "Clear Data." 3. Restart your Samsung Galaxy S6. 4. If the problem continues, uninstall and then reinstall the Skype app from the Google Play Store.
cq_1: Does the microphone work correctly for other applications on your Samsung Galaxy S6, such as voice recorders or other calling apps?
user_response_1: I haven't checked that. I only know the headphones work for listening to things like YouTube on my phone, but I haven't tried the microphone on any other phone apps.
solution_1: Since you haven't tested the microphone with other apps on your phone, please first try using your Samsung Galaxy S6's built-in voice recorder app or another calling application to confirm if the microphone captures your voice. If it works, then proceed with checking Skype's microphone permissions, clearing its cache and data, and finally reinstalling the Skype app. If the microphone doesn't work with other apps, the issue may be device-related.
cq_2: Could you please test the microphone using your phone's built-in voice recorder app or another calling app to see if it captures your voice?
user_response_2: I haven't checked. I'm not sure about that.
solution_2: To effectively troubleshoot, it's essential to first confirm if your microphone works with any other application on your Samsung Galaxy S6. If it does, then proceed with checking Skype's microphone permissions, clearing its cache and data, and reinstalling the app. If the microphone fails to work with other apps as well, the issue likely lies with your phone's hardware or system settings, indicating a broader device problem rather than just Skype.
cq_3: Have you ever successfully used the microphone on your Samsung Galaxy S6 for any purpose other than Skype, such as recording a voice note or making a regular phone call?
user_response_3: I haven't checked. I'm not sure about using it for regular phone calls or voice notes.
final_solution: To resolve your issue, first, test your microphone with another app on your Samsung Galaxy S6, like the built-in voice recorder or a regular phone call. If it works, then proceed with these steps for Skype: 1. Go to Settings > Apps > Skype > Permissions and enable Microphone. 2. Clear Skype's cache and data (Settings > Apps > Skype > Storage). 3. Reinstall Skype. If the microphone doesn't work with any app, the issue is likely with your phone's hardware.
accepted_answer: Hello DeborahLow,  Thank you for the follow up.  I already send a private message at your inbox here in Skype Community, let us continue our conversation there, so that we can further assist you.  Thanks.  Regards,

title: Convert picture to drawing
original_question: I have a jpg of a caricature drawing, and I would like to convert it back to a drawing.  I remember doing this some time ago (probably an earlier version of PowerPoint), but I can't seem to remember how in Office 2010.  I thought it was "Edit Drawing" "Edit points", or something, but I can't find the command now.  Can anyone remind me how this is done in Office 2010?  I also have 2013 if necessary. Thanks,
preliminary_solution: PowerPoint does not have a built-in feature to convert a standard JPG image directly into an editable drawing with points. The "Ungroup" or "Convert to Shape" functionality you recall typically applies to vector image formats (like WMF or EMF files). If your image is a vector file, insert it, then select it and go to Picture Tools Format > Ungroup (you may need to click it twice). If it's a JPG, you would need to manually trace it using PowerPoint's drawing tools or use an external vectorization program.
cq_1: Was the original caricature drawing you previously converted to an editable drawing in PowerPoint a JPG file, or was it a different image format like a WMF or EMF?
user_response_1: I'm not sure about that. I just remember using those commands in an older version of PowerPoint. The picture I'm trying to change right now is definitely a JPG, though.
solution_1: Since your current image is a JPG, PowerPoint cannot directly convert it into an editable drawing with points. The "Edit Drawing" and "Edit Points" commands you recall are typically used for vector image formats (like WMF or EMF files), which can be ungrouped into editable shapes. For your JPG, you would need to manually trace the caricature using PowerPoint's drawing tools or utilize an external vectorization program to achieve an editable drawing.
cq_2: What specific modifications or edits do you intend to make to the caricature once it's converted to a drawing?
user_response_2: I'm not sure about that. I just know my main goal is to turn the picture into line art.
solution_2: Since your goal is to turn the JPG into line art, PowerPoint doesn't offer a direct conversion for this. You can, however, apply artistic effects to the JPG to give it a line drawing appearance. For truly editable line art, you would need to manually trace the caricature using PowerPoint's drawing tools (shapes, freeform lines) or use an external program to convert the JPG to a vector format before importing it into PowerPoint.
cq_3: Are you looking for a quick visual effect that resembles line art, or do you need the image to be composed of editable lines and shapes within PowerPoint?
user_response_3: I think I need it to be editable lines and shapes. I was looking for the 'Edit points' tool that I used in an older version so I could actually change the drawing.
final_solution: To achieve editable lines and shapes from your JPG in PowerPoint, direct conversion using 'Edit Points' is not possible. The 'Edit Points' tool works on shapes created within PowerPoint or imported vector files. Your best options are: 1. Manually trace the caricature using PowerPoint's 'Freeform' or 'Curve' tools. The resulting shapes will be fully editable with 'Edit Points'. 2. Use an external vectorization program to convert the JPG to a vector format (like EMF or WMF), then insert it into PowerPoint. Some vector formats can be ungrouped into editable shapes.
accepted_answer: PowerPoint's never been able to convert raster images (like your JPG) to vector.   Raster = a collection of variously colored dots that happen to look like a picture Vector = mathematically defined lines/text/shapes that make up a picture  PowerPoint and many other programs can convert vector graphics to raster but it takes a tracing program to go the other way.  PowerPoint doesn't do it ... sorry.

title: Calling to India
original_question: i am using skype subscription to india . when i am calling they canot hear my voice but i can hear them . i am using skype in iphone 6  please help me solve the problem
